---
title: "OpenClaw Memory Index"
format: gfm
---

# OpenClaw Memory Index

This file is a durable, human-readable memory index in QMD format.
It exists to make future recall easier by giving important operating context a stable home outside of raw daily notes.

## How to use this file
- Put durable patterns here, not every transient event.
- Keep daily chronology in `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`.
- Use this file for:
  - stable operating rules
  - long-lived workflow expectations
  - room/agent ownership
  - recurring decisions worth remembering

## Durable workflow memory

### Engineering and QA
- Engineering is the live implementation room.
- Engineering can look at other rooms for context when asked to do work originating there.
- Engineering work is not considered complete just because coding finished.
- Default completion loop:

  Engineering -> QA -> Engineering (if needed) -> QA -> Done/Approval

- QA is the review gate for code, config, automation, integration, and other engineering-impact work.

### Room ownership
- `engineering` -> `engineering`
- `signal-and-circuit` -> `signal-and-circuit`
- `command-center` -> `command-center`
- `ops-desk` -> `ops-desk`
- `support-inbox` -> `support-inbox`
- `caruso-growth` -> `caruso-growth`
- `caruso-product` -> `caruso-product`
- `security-infra` -> `security-infra`
- `research-lab` -> `research-lab`
- `qa-review` -> `qa-review` role/function

### Canonical reference docs
- `ROOMS_AND_WORKFLOW_SPEC.md`
- `ENGINEERING_ROOM_GUIDE.md`
- `ENGINEERING_QA_LOOP.md`

## Updating guidance
Update this file when:
- room ownership changes
- the engineering/QA workflow changes
- a new durable operating rule is adopted
- an implementation pattern becomes the normal expected workflow
